title: Biz Stone

infobox.value: Christopher Isaac Stone   (1974-03-10) March 10, 1974 (age 52)  [Boston](/wiki/Boston "Boston"), Massachusetts, US
[Northeastern University](/wiki/Northeastern_University "Northeastern University") [University of Massachusetts Boston](/wiki/University_of_Massachusetts_Boston "University of Massachusetts Boston")

"**Biz**" **Stone**[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-noemail-2) (born March 10, 1974)[[3]](#cite_note-3) is an American entrepreneur who is a co-founder of [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter"), among other tech companies. Stone was the creative director at [Xanga](/wiki/Xanga "Xanga") from 1999 to 2001.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Stone co-founded [Jelly](/wiki/Jelly_(app) "Jelly (app)"), with Ben Finkel. Jelly was launched in 2014 and was a search engine driven by visual imagery and discovery. Stone was Jelly's CEO until its [acquisition](/wiki/Mergers_and_acquisitions "Mergers and acquisitions") by [Pinterest](/wiki/Pinterest "Pinterest") in 2017.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

["How I Got My Name"](https://web.archive.org/web/20120122112152/http://www.bizstone.com/2004/11/how-i-got-my-name.html). Archived from [the original](http://www.bizstone.com/2004/11/how-i-got-my-name.html) on January 22, 2012. Retrieved February 6, 2012.

Biz Stone (April 12, 2000). ["Biz Stone:No Emails Today"](https://web.archive.org/web/20100213184210/http://www.bizstone.com/2000/04/no-emails-today-except-couple-work.html). Archived from [the original](http://www.bizstone.com/2000/04/no-emails-today-except-couple-work.html) on February 13, 2010. Retrieved February 15, 2009.

Biz Stone [@biz] (March 10, 2010). ["Today is my birthday—I'm in my 30's!"](https://twitter.com/biz/status/10295408614) ([Tweet](/wiki/Tweet_(social_media) "Tweet (social media)")) – via [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter").
Stone, Biz (March 8, 2017). ["Big News—Pinterest Acquires Jelly!"](https://blog.jelly.co/big-news-pinterest-acquires-jelly-1ef68d36480d). *AskJelly.com News*. Retrieved January 23, 2019.
Graham, Natalie (August 29, 2014). ["Biz Stone: Making big money from the little bird"](https://www.ft.com/content/052b67fa-0690-11e4-ba32-00144feab7de). *Financial Times*.
["Who should be the 2009 Bostonian of the Year?"](http://www.boston.com/bostonglobe/magazine/galleries/2009/1220/BOTYpoll?pg=8). *The Boston Globe*. December 14, 2009.

["Twitter's Biz Stone To Be Executive Fellow At UC Berkeley's Haas School"](http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2011/09/02/twitters-biz-stone-uc-berkeley-haas_n_946391.html). *The Huffington Post*. September 21, 2011.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American blogger and co-founder of Twitter (born 1974)

| Biz Stone | |
| --- | --- |
| Stone in 2025 | |
| Born | Christopher Isaac Stone   (1974-03-10) March 10, 1974 (age 52)  [Boston](/wiki/Boston "Boston"), Massachusetts, US |
| Education | [Northeastern University](/wiki/Northeastern_University "Northeastern University") [University of Massachusetts Boston](/wiki/University_of_Massachusetts_Boston "University of Massachusetts Boston") |
| Occupations | [Creative director](/wiki/Creative_director "Creative director"), [software engineer](/wiki/Software_engineer "Software engineer"), [businessperson](/wiki/Businessperson "Businessperson") |
| Known for | co-founder of [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter"), [Jelly](/wiki/Jelly_(app) "Jelly (app)") |
| Spouse | Livia Stone |

**Christopher Isaac** "**Biz**" **Stone**[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-noemail-2) (born March 10, 1974)[[3]](#cite_note-3) is an American entrepreneur who is a co-founder of [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter"), among other tech companies. Stone was the creative director at [Xanga](/wiki/Xanga "Xanga") from 1999 to 2001.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Stone co-founded [Jelly](/wiki/Jelly_(app) "Jelly (app)"), with Ben Finkel. Jelly was launched in 2014 and was a search engine driven by visual imagery and discovery. Stone was Jelly's CEO until its [acquisition](/wiki/Mergers_and_acquisitions "Mergers and acquisitions") by [Pinterest](/wiki/Pinterest "Pinterest") in 2017.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Biz_Stone&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Education")]

Stone was born in [Boston](/wiki/Boston "Boston"), Massachusetts, in 1974.[[6]](#cite_note-6) He graduated from [Wellesley High School](/wiki/Wellesley_High_School "Wellesley High School") in [Wellesley, Massachusetts](/wiki/Wellesley,_Massachusetts "Wellesley, Massachusetts").[[7]](#cite_note-7) He attended [Northeastern University](/wiki/Northeastern_University "Northeastern University") and the [University of Massachusetts Boston](/wiki/University_of_Massachusetts_Boston "University of Massachusetts Boston"), but did not graduate from either.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Biz_Stone&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/62/Biz_addressing_NCC_2010.jpg/250px-Biz_addressing_NCC_2010.jpg)](/wiki/File:Biz_addressing_NCC_2010.jpg)

Stone addressing a conference in 2010

Stone started his career moving boxes at [Little, Brown and Company](/wiki/Little,_Brown_and_Company "Little, Brown and Company") while at university in Boston. He was promoted as a book cover designer and left school to work full-time.[[9]](#cite_note-:0-9)[[10]](#cite_note-:1-10)

From 1999 to 2001, Stone was creative director at [Xanga](/wiki/Xanga "Xanga").[[9]](#cite_note-:0-9)[[10]](#cite_note-:1-10)

He quit Xanga and got a job at [Wellesley College](/wiki/Wellesley_College "Wellesley College")[[9]](#cite_note-:0-9) for about a year before being invited to work for Google's [Blogger](/wiki/Blogger_(service) "Blogger (service)") team.

From 2003 to 2005, Stone held a senior leadership role at [Google](/wiki/Google "Google").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Stone co-founded the social network [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter") in 2006 and left the company in 2011.[[12]](#cite_note-rejoin-12)

Stone made his [directorial](/wiki/Film_director "Film director") debut in 2012, working alongside [Ron Howard](/wiki/Ron_Howard "Ron Howard") and [Canon USA](/wiki/Canon_USA "Canon USA") to direct a short film, Evermore, as a part of Project Imaginat10n.[[13]](#cite_note-13) Stone is also an advisor to Zoic Studios, and an Executive Producer of the documentary *[Eating Animals](/wiki/Eating_Animals "Eating Animals")* along with [Natalie Portman](/wiki/Natalie_Portman "Natalie Portman").[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Stone returned to [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter") in 2017[[12]](#cite_note-rejoin-12) where he remained till 2021.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)

In 2024, Stone joined the board of directors of social network [Mastodon](/wiki/Mastodon_(social_network) "Mastodon (social network)")'s US nonprofit entity.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

Aside from Twitter, Stone is an [angel investor](/wiki/Angel_investor "Angel investor") and advisor in the startup community having backed companies such as [Square](/wiki/Square,_Inc. "Square, Inc."), [Slack](/wiki/Slack_(software) "Slack (software)"), [Medium](/wiki/Medium_(website) "Medium (website)"), [Nest](/wiki/Nest_Labs "Nest Labs"), [Beyond Meat](/wiki/Beyond_Meat "Beyond Meat"), [Pinterest](/wiki/Pinterest "Pinterest"), [Intercom](/wiki/Intercom_(company) "Intercom (company)"), and Faraday. Stone is a board director at Beyond Meat, Medium, Polaroid Swing, Workpop, and Jelly Industries. Stone is the Chairman of Polaroid Swing.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

## Awards and honors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Biz_Stone&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Awards and honors")]

Along with [Jack Dorsey](/wiki/Jack_Dorsey "Jack Dorsey"), Stone holds the patent for inventing Twitter.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

Stone has been honored with the [International Center for Journalists](/wiki/International_Center_for_Journalists "International Center for Journalists") Innovation Award, [*Inc.*](/wiki/Inc._Magazine "Inc. Magazine") magazine named him Entrepreneur of the Decade, [*Time*](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)") listed him as one of the [100 Most Influential People in the World](/wiki/Time_100 "Time 100"),[[20]](#cite_note-20) and *[GQ](/wiki/GQ "GQ")* named him Nerd of the Year, along with [Evan Williams](/wiki/Evan_Williams_(Internet_entrepreneur) "Evan Williams (Internet entrepreneur)").[[21]](#cite_note-21) In 2014, *[The Economist](/wiki/The_Economist "The Economist")* recognized Stone with an Innovation Award.

In 2015, Stone's Twitter won an [Emmy](/wiki/Emmy "Emmy") and Stone received [CIPR's](/wiki/CIPR "CIPR") most prestigious accolade for leadership at the forefront of developing new forms of media.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

Stone is a visiting fellow at [Oxford University](/wiki/Oxford_University "Oxford University") and a member of the Senior Common Room at [Exeter College, Oxford](/wiki/Exeter_College,_Oxford "Exeter College, Oxford"). Upon delivering the 2011 commencement, [Babson College](/wiki/Babson_College "Babson College") awarded Stone their highest honorary degree, a Doctor of Laws.[[23]](#cite_note-23) and is a Fellow[[24]](#cite_note-24) at [Oxford University](/wiki/Oxford_University "Oxford University").[[25]](#cite_note-25) Stone is an Executive Fellow at [University of California, Berkeley](/wiki/University_of_California,_Berkeley "University of California, Berkeley").[[26]](#cite_note-26)

## Published works

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Biz_Stone&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Published works")]

Stone has published three books,

* *Blogging: Genius Strategies for Instant Web Content* (New Riders, 2002),
* *Who Let The Blogs Out?* (St Martins, 2004),[[27]](#cite_note-27) and
* *Things A Little Bird Told Me* (Grand Central, 2014).[[28]](#cite_note-28)[[29]](#cite_note-29)

In addition to his long-running personal [blog](/wiki/Blog "Blog") and Medium articles, Stone has published [op-eds](/wiki/Op-ed "Op-ed") for *[The Atlantic](/wiki/The_Atlantic "The Atlantic")*[[30]](#cite_note-30) and [*The Times*](/wiki/The_Times_of_London "The Times of London").

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Biz_Stone&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Stone is Jewish.[[31]](#cite_note-31) He is involved in [animal rights](/wiki/Animal_rights "Animal rights"), [veganism](/wiki/Veganism "Veganism"),[[32]](#cite_note-32) [environmentalism](/wiki/Environmentalism "Environmentalism"), [poverty](/wiki/Poverty "Poverty"), [health](/wiki/Health "Health") and [education](/wiki/Education "Education").[[33]](#cite_note-33) Stone is an advisor and contributor to [DonorsChoose](/wiki/DonorsChoose "DonorsChoose"), a non-profit organization helping classrooms in need.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

Stone lives in [Marin County, California](/wiki/Marin_County,_California "Marin County, California"), with his wife Livia and his son Jacob.[[35]](#cite_note-35) He and his wife founded and operate the Biz and Livia Stone Foundation, which supports education and conservation in California.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Biz_Stone&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Jelly (app)](/wiki/Jelly_(app) "Jelly (app)")
